pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates document at line 242: doc.save(output_path) where output_path='/home/ga/Documents/clinical_overview_draft.docx'
export_result.sh references OUTPUT_PATH at line 12 and task.json description specifies 'save the document as /home/ga/Documents/clinical_overview_formatted.docx'

setup_task.sh creates exactly 2 page breaks (lines 113 and 158), which when converted to section breaks produce 3 total sections: Section 1 (Title/TOC/Executive Summary), Section 2 (2.5.1), Section 3 (2.5.2-2.5.3-References)
task.json description lines specify header as 'Sorvimab — NDA 215847 — Clinical Overview' for Section 2 and Section 3

pi_items.metadata_value: Anti-PD-L1 humanized monoclonal antibody for NSCLC by Meridian Biotherapeutics, NDA 215847
pi_items.verified_value: FICTIONAL - no results found in FDA database or drug registries
pi_items.source: Web searches for 'Sorvimab NDA 215847', 'Sorvimab anti-PD-L1', and 'FDA NDA 215847' returned no matching results. Similar-sounding 'Sotrovimab' is a real drug but is a COVID-19 monoclonal antibody by GSK/Vir, not an anti-PD-L1 for NSCLC, and was granted Emergency Use Authorization (not standard NDA) with EUA canceled in April 2022.
Web searches for 'FDA NDA 215847' and specific NDA database queries returned no results matching this application number

pi_items.metadata_value: Meridian Biotherapeutics, Inc., 350 Innovation Drive, Cambridge, MA 02142
pi_items.verified_value: FICTIONAL - Meridian Bioscience exists but is headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio and has no connection to 'Sorvimab'
pi_items.source: Web search for 'Meridian Biotherapeutics Cambridge Massachusetts' and 'Meridian Biotherapeutics NDA FDA' found real company Meridian Bioscience in Cincinnati, Ohio, involved with diagnostic products and ML141 (docetaxel formulation), not with any immunotherapy drug named Sorvimab. The Cambridge, MA address appears fabricated.

pi_items.source: Parsing setup_task.sh reveals section numbering created at lines 161 (2.5.1), 189 (2.5.2), and 207 (2.5.3). References section created at line 225. No sections 2.5.4, 2.5.5, or 2.5.6 are present.

pi_items.verified_value: approximately 5-6 pages (estimate based on content volume in setup_task.sh: title page ~0.5p, TOC ~0.5p, executive summary ~1p, 2.5.1 ~0.5p, 2.5.2 ~0.5p, 2.5.3 ~0.5p, references ~0.5p = ~5.5p total)
pi_items.source: Content analysis of setup_task.sh paragraphs and structure estimate

privileged_info_summary: The task involves a completely synthetic document created programmatically by setup_task.sh using python-docx. The draft document contains 39 paragraphs structured with 2 page breaks creating 3 sections: Title page/TOC/Executive Summary (Section 1), 2.5.1 Product Development Rationale (Section 2), and 2.5.2-2.5.3-References (Section 3). The output path is correctly specified as /home/ga/Documents/clinical_overview_formatted.docx and the required section count of 3 is accurate. However, the drug 'Sorvimab' does not exist in FDA databases; a similar 'Sotrovimab' exists but is a COVID-19 monoclonal antibody by GSK, not an anti-PD-L1 immunotherapy for NSCLC. NDA number 215847 appears fabricated (no FDA database records), as does the manufacturer address (Meridian Bioscience is real but Ohio-based, not involved with this fictional drug). The task description incorrectly claims sections 2.5.1–2.5.6 when only 2.5.1–2.5.3 are created. The header text metadata is accurately specified and matches the task requirements.
